prompt: Updating a Swing component from a background thread causes intermittent display bugs. What is the bug?
choices: JLabel.setText is synchronized and thread-safe for any thread
Swing components are not thread-safe; mutating label.setText from a non-EDT thread violates the single-thread rule and can cause rendering corruption or missed updates
new Thread() must call Thread.sleep(100) before updating Swing to ensure the EDT is ready
setVisible(true) must be called on the EDT for this to matter
explanation: Swing is single-threaded. All reads and writes to Swing components must happen on the Event Dispatch Thread. Calling label.setText() from a plain new Thread() violates this and can cause inconsistent rendering, missed repaints, or exceptions. Fix: wrap the update in SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> label.setText("done"));
